name: Movie Quotes
description: Iconic lines from film — match the quote to the movie

questions.q: 'I'll have what she's having' is an iconic line from which romantic comedy?

source.notes: 1989 film, directed by Rob Reiner. Said by Estelle Reiner (Rob's mother) after Sally's famous deli scene.

questions.q: 'Say hello to my little friend!' is from which 1983 crime film?

source.notes: Spoken by Al Pacino as Tony Montana. 'Little friend' is an M203 grenade launcher.

questions.q: 'You had me at hello' is a famous line from which 1996 film?

source.notes: Said by Renée Zellweger's character. Cuba Gooding Jr.'s 'Show me the money!' is from the same film.

questions.q: 'I'm having an old friend for dinner' is uttered by Hannibal Lecter in which thriller?

source.notes: Film ends with Lecter speaking this to Clarice on a payphone.

questions.q: 'Mrs. Robinson, you're trying to seduce me' is from which 1967 film?
choices: In the Heat of the Night

source.notes: Dustin Hoffman says it to Anne Bancroft. Followed by '...aren't you?'

questions.q: 'If you're a bird, I'm a bird' is from which romantic drama?

source.notes: Spoken by Ryan Gosling to Rachel McAdams. 2004 film based on Nicholas Sparks novel.

questions.q: 'Funny how? I mean funny like I'm a clown, I amuse you?' is Joe Pesci's iconic line from which film?

source.notes: Martin Scorsese film (1990). Pesci won Best Supporting Actor.

questions.q: 'May the Force be with you' is the signature line from which franchise?

source.notes: First said by General Dodonna in 'A New Hope' (1977). May 4th is 'Star Wars Day.'

questions.q: 'I am your father' is spoken by which character in 'The Empire Strikes Back' (1980)?

source.notes: Voiced by James Earl Jones. Often misquoted as 'Luke, I am your father.'

questions.q: 'Here's looking at you, kid' is spoken by which character in 'Casablanca'?

source.notes: Humphrey Bogart to Ingrid Bergman. Not in the script — ad-libbed by Bogart.

questions.q: 'I see dead people' is the iconic line from which 1999 film?

source.notes: Haley Joel Osment whispers it to Bruce Willis. Directed by M. Night Shyamalan.

questions.q: 'You can't handle the truth!' is a famous courtroom line from which film?

source.notes: 1992 film. Jack Nicholson as Colonel Jessup, shouting at Tom Cruise's character.

questions.q: 'Show me the money!' is a memorable line from which Tom Cruise film?

source.notes: Spoken by Cuba Gooding Jr., who won Best Supporting Actor.

questions.q: 'E.T. phone home' is from which 1982 film?
choices: Close Encounters of the Third Kind

source.notes: Directed by Steven Spielberg. Highest-grossing film of the 1980s.

questions.q: 'Life is like a box of chocolates. You never know what you're gonna get' is from which film?

source.notes: Tom Hanks as the title character. Won Best Picture 1995.

questions.q: 'Frankly, my dear, I don't give a damn' is famously spoken by Rhett Butler in which 1939 epic?

source.notes: Final line of the film. Clark Gable as Rhett. The line caused controversy over 'damn.'

questions.q: 'To infinity and beyond!' is the catchphrase of which Pixar character?

source.notes: Space Ranger from 'Toy Story' (1995). Voiced by Tim Allen.

questions.q: 'Bond. James Bond' has been the 007 introduction since which film?

source.notes: First film in the series (1962). Sean Connery played Bond.

questions.q: 'Hasta la vista, baby' is spoken by the Terminator in which 1991 sequel?

source.notes: T-800 learns it from John Connor. Directed by James Cameron.

questions.q: 'There's no place like home' is said by Dorothy in which classic film?

source.notes: Dorothy Gale clicks her ruby slippers three times. 1939 film starring Judy Garland.
